hypothesis_B.rationale: Places biological/balneological before zodiac (body → stars logic)

hypothesis_C.rationale: Places cosmological rosettes at manuscript center

hypothesis_D.rationale: Groups all plant/remedy sections together before theory sections

prefix_correlation.interpretation: WEAK CORRELATION: Prefix distributions are relatively uniform across sections. Prefixes may be primarily cipher artifacts rather than grammatical markers.

core_isolation.interpretation: SIGNIFICANT: Removing positional affixes INCREASES entropy, meaning the affixes were suppressing entropy. This is consistent with affixes being low-entropy grammatical markers and cores carrying higher-entropy cipher content.

cross_section_consistency.interpretation: Found 0 parameter sets that match across multiple zodiac sections. WEAK evidence for a consistent cipher configuration.

findings.finding: Cold start patterns detected at scribe transitions
findings.implication: Cipher uses resettable state (supports Naibbe dice mechanism)
Cipher uses progressive state; correct page order is critical

findings.finding: Positional affixes are a separable layer (ΔH2=+0.2024)
findings.evidence: SIGNIFICANT: Removing positional affixes INCREASES entropy, meaning the affixes were suppressing entropy. This is consistent with affixes being low-entropy grammatical markers and cores carrying higher-entropy cipher content.
findings.implication: Decrypt should target semantic cores, not full tokens
These high-frequency uniform words likely encode grammar, not content

constraints.description: Cipher output must match Voynich entropy profile
Cipher must be operable by 5 distinct scribes with consistent output
constraints.implication: System must use shared, standardized tables

constraints.description: Certain glyphs must be restricted to word-initial/final positions
Cipher mechanism must be executable with 1400-1438 CE technology
constraints.implication: Only hand-operable methods: tables, dice, cards, grilles

constraints.description: Source language must produce Voynich-like entropy when encrypted
constraints.evidence: Romance month labels, swallowtail merlons, alpine botanical style

pro: Zodiac month labels in Romance language suggest Latin-sphere origin
Pharmaceutical recipe format matches Latin medical tradition
Hartlieb-era medical Latin vocabulary fits temporal/geographic window
Latin was the universal scholarly language of 15th-century medicine
Swallowtail merlons in cosmological section = Northern Italian architecture
Month labels may be Northern French/Occitan (adjacent language area)
Balneological tradition strong in Northern Italy (Balneis Puteolanis)
Hartlieb was Bavarian, writing in German and Latin
Alpine botanical style consistent with German-speaking regions
Low entropy could be consistent with German compound words
contra: German word structure less compatible with observed word-length distribution

confidence_assessment.message: Multiple high-confidence findings converge on a consistent cipher model. The combined constraints significantly narrow the solution space.
